title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
JavaScript is single-threaded — it can only do one thing at a time. The event loop is the mechanism that allows JS to perform non-blocking operations despite this.

Components:
• Call Stack — where function calls live. Only one frame executes at a time.
• Web APIs / Node APIs — handle async work (timers, fetch, I/O) outside the call stack.
• Callback Queue (Macrotask Queue) — completed async callbacks wait here (setTimeout, setInterval, I/O).
• Microtask Queue — higher-priority queue for Promise .then() callbacks and queueMicrotask().
• Event Loop — continuously checks: if call stack is empty, push the next microtask (all of them first), then the next macrotask.

Microtasks always run BEFORE the next macrotask.
